column1: # grep-tells.tsv — TIER 1 (flat-presence) rules for audit-swiftui-document-picker-permissions (dp-01 … dp-07).
# severity: hard | warn | adv   (hard → runner exits 2). The runner greps each ERE over the target *.swift.
# This LOCATES only — the LLM agent READS each hit (SKILL.md step 3) and decides. Structural rules that
# grep cannot express (start-without-stop balance, NSItemProvider-inside-onDrop) live in ast-grep/*.yml (tier 2).
# NOTE: dp-07 (Info.plist usage string) is READ BY HAND from Info.plist in ORIENT — the runner is
# *.swift-only; the dp-07 tell below only LOCATES the Photos/Files APIs that REQUIRE a usage string.

column4: file read/write — REPORT only if the URL is a PICKED, out-of-container URL (from fileImporter/UIDocumentPickerViewController) read with NO preceding startAccessingSecurityScopedResource() (dp-01). READ the file: own-container/Bundle.main reads need no scope.
bookmarkData — REPORT if it lacks options: .withSecurityScope (a plain bookmark round-trips the path, NOT the security scope) (dp-02). READ the call.
a picked URL persisted by .path/.absoluteString — the scope is gone next launch → security-scoped bookmark Data (dp-02)
security-scoped access — VERIFY a balancing stopAccessingSecurityScopedResource()/defer in the same scope, and that the returned Bool is checked (dp-03; ref-count leak otherwise)

a raw UIKit picker bridge — VERIFY whether SwiftUI fileImporter/PhotosPicker fits instead (dp-04, advisory; WHETHER-to-bridge owned by uikit-overuse → cross_ref). A filter SwiftUI lacks can justify it.
manual NSItemProvider/onDrop drag-drop — prefer Transferable + .draggable/.dropDestination(for:) with a Sendable type (dp-05)

loadTransferable — if it touches a @MainActor-created PhotosPickerItem inside a nonisolated .task, Swift-6 data race (dp-06); ISOLATION fix owned by concurrency-safety → cross_ref. Confirm consent/bookmark of the result if it is a file.

a Photos/Files API in use — VERIFY a matching Info.plist usage string exists (NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription/NSCameraUsageDescription, read plist by hand) (dp-07, advisory; usage string owned by privacy-permissions → cross_ref)
